questions_by_commandment.title: I am the Lord your God: you shall not have strange Gods before me.
questions: Have I doubted or denied the existence of God?
Have I put money, career, status, or technology before God?
Have I engaged in superstitious practices, magic, or occultism?
Have I failed to pray daily?

questions_by_commandment.title: You shall not take the name of the Lord your God in vain.
questions: Have I used God's name in anger or carelessly?
Have I spoken disrespectfully about the Church or holy things?
Have I broken a solemn vow, oath, or promise to God?

questions_by_commandment.title: Remember to keep holy the Lord's Day.
questions: Have I deliberately missed Mass on Sundays or Holy Days of Obligation?
Have I treated Sunday as a day like any other, without rest or worship?
Have I received Holy Communion while in a state of mortal sin?
Have I been distracted or disrespectful during Mass?

questions_by_commandment.title: Honor your father and your mother.
questions: Have I been disrespectful, rebellious, or ungrateful to my parents or elders?
Have I neglected to care for my aging parents or relatives?
Have I failed in my duty to correct or spiritually nurture my children?

questions_by_commandment.title: You shall not kill.
questions: Have I held anger, hatred, or resentment toward another person?
Have I been physically or verbally abusive?
Have I encouraged, supported, or participated in abortion, euthanasia, or suicide?
Have I harmed my body with drugs, excessive alcohol, or reckless behavior?

questions_by_commandment.title: You shall not commit adultery.
questions: Have I been unfaithful to my spouse in thought, word, or action?
Have I engaged in sexual activity outside of marriage?
Have I viewed pornography or impure images?
Have I dressed or acted in a way that incited lustful thoughts in others?

questions_by_commandment.title: You shall not steal.
questions: Have I stolen, cheated, or committed fraud, no matter how small?
Have I failed to return borrowed items or money?
Have I been dishonest in my work, taxes, or business dealings?
Have I been wasteful of resources or failed to help the poor?

questions_by_commandment.title: You shall not bear false witness against your neighbor.
questions: Have I told lies, even small ones?
Have I gossiped or harmed the reputation of others?
Have I been quick to judge others without knowing the full truth?
Have I refused to admit when I was wrong or apologize?

questions_by_commandment.title: You shall not covet your neighbor's wife.
questions: Have I entertained impure thoughts or desires for someone else's spouse?
Have I used others as objects in my imagination?

questions_by_commandment.title: You shall not covet your neighbor's goods.
questions: Have I been envious of what others possess (their money, talents, or happiness)?
Have I complained excessively about what I lack instead of being grateful?

specific_states.title: For Married Couples
questions: Have I failed to show love and support to my spouse today?
Have I been selfish or insisted on my own way in the home?
Have I failed to be open to new life within our marriage?
Have I neglected the spiritual or moral education of our children?
Have I held grudges against my spouse instead of forgiving?

specific_states.title: For Single Adults
questions: Have I used my time and freedom solely for myself rather than in service to others?
Have I been impatient with my current state in life?
Have I failed to practice chastity in my relationships?
Have I neglected my spiritual growth or service to the Church?

specific_states.title: For Teenagers
questions: Have I been disobedient or talked back to my parents?
Have I been lazy in my schoolwork or chores?
Have I joined in when others were bullying or mocking someone?
Have I experimented with drugs, alcohol, or vaping?
Have I been honest with my parents about where I am and who I'm with?
Have I used social media to hurt others or viewed inappropriate content?

alternative_exams.title: Examen based on the Beatitudes
alternative_exams.description: A deeper examen that measures the disposition of the heart toward holiness and the love of God.

categories.name: The poor in spirit
categories.focus: Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
questions: Do I recognize my total need for God, or do I rely only on my own strength and successes?
Have I let pride or self-sufficiency pull me away from prayer?
Am I detached from material goods, or does my happiness depend on what I own?

categories.name: Those who mourn
categories.focus: Blessed are those who mourn, for they shall be comforted.
questions: Do I grieve my sin and the harm I cause others, or am I indifferent?
Have I sought comfort in vices or worldly distractions instead of seeking peace in God?
Am I able to accompany and console those who suffer around me?

categories.name: The meek
categories.focus: Blessed are the meek, for they shall inherit the earth.
questions: Have I been arrogant or impatient with those I considered 'beneath' me?
Do I try to impose my will at all costs in my family or at work?
Do I accept criticism with humility, or do I react with anger and defensiveness?

categories.name: Hunger and thirst for righteousness
categories.focus: Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they shall be satisfied.
questions: Do I seek God's will first in my daily decisions?
Do I make an effort to grow in my faith and in my knowledge of the Word of God?
Am I indifferent to social injustice or to the poverty of others?

categories.name: The merciful
categories.focus: Blessed are the merciful, for they shall obtain mercy.
questions: Do I hold grudges, or do I find it hard to forgive those who have offended me?
Have I judged others harshly without knowing their struggles?
Do I carry out works of mercy (feeding the hungry, visiting the sick, consoling)?

categories.name: The pure in heart
categories.focus: Blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God.
questions: Have I consented to impure thoughts, words, or acts?
Are my intentions upright, or do I seek recognition for myself in my good works?
Do I guard what I see and hear so as to keep my mind focused on what is holy?

categories.name: The peacemakers
categories.focus: Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall be called children of God.
questions: Have I caused divisions, gossip, or conflicts in my home or community?
Do I seek to reconcile people, or do I enjoy arguments?
Do I have peace in my heart, or do I live in a constant state of anxiety and distrust?

categories.name: Those persecuted
categories.focus: Blessed are those who are persecuted for the sake of righteousness, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
questions: Am I ashamed of my faith or of my Christian values in front of my friends or colleagues?
Have I stopped doing what is right out of fear of 'what others will say'?
Am I willing to sacrifice my comfort to defend the truth and the Gospel?

alternative_exams.title: Examen of the Threefold Love
alternative_exams.description: A review based on the Great Commandment: love God, neighbor, and oneself.

categories.name: Love of God
categories.focus: You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, and with all your mind.
questions: Have I sought God today for who He is, or only for what He can give me?
Have I given quality time to prayer, or only offered Him the 'leftovers' of my time?
Have I trusted in His love during difficulties, or rebelled against His will?
Have I received the Sacraments with reverence and a desire for conversion?

categories.name: Love of Neighbor
categories.focus: You shall love your neighbor as yourself.
questions: Have I treated others with the dignity they deserve as children of God?
Have I been quick to judge and slow to forgive?
Have I shared my goods, my time, or my talents with those most in need?
Have I harmed anyone with my words, gossip, or indifferent silences?
Have I fulfilled my duties toward my family, being patient and of service?

categories.name: Love of self
categories.focus: The care of the temple of the Holy Spirit that you are.
questions: Do I treat myself with the mercy with which God treats me, or am I excessively harsh and perfectionistic?
Have I neglected my health, rest, or nutrition irresponsibly?
Have I allowed thoughts of low self-esteem or worthlessness to dominate my mind?
Do I strive to cultivate my talents to put them at God's service, or do I bury them out of fear or laziness?
Have I guarded the purity of my heart, mind, and body?

alternative_exams.title: Examen of the Seven Capital Sins
alternative_exams.description: An analysis of the roots of our faults to identify our main weaknesses.

categories.name: Pride
categories.focus: The disordered desire to be preferred above others.
questions: Have I been boastful, or sought others' admiration constantly?
Do I find it hard to ask forgiveness or admit I am wrong?
Have I looked down on others in my mind because of their economic, social, or intellectual situation?
Do I take credit for my successes, forgetting they are gifts from God?

categories.name: Greed
categories.focus: The disordered desire to possess wealth.
questions: Am I excessively attached to money or to my belongings?
Have I been stingy with the needy or with my own family?
Do I spend money on unnecessary luxuries while neglecting my Christian duties?
Have I placed my security in money instead of in God?

categories.name: Lust
categories.focus: The disordered desire for sexual pleasure.
questions: Have I sought sexual pleasure through pornography or masturbation?
Have I voluntarily consented to impure thoughts or desires?
Have I disrespected others' dignity by seeing them as objects?
Have I taken part in conversations or jokes of double meaning that offend purity?

categories.name: Envy
categories.focus: Sadness at the good of another.
questions: Have I felt sadness or bitterness over others' success or happiness?
Have I wished someone would fail so that I could feel better about myself?
Have I criticized others out of jealousy to diminish their reputation?
Do I suffer when someone I dislike receives recognition?

categories.name: Gluttony
categories.focus: The disordered desire to eat and drink.
questions: Have I eaten or drunk excessively, to the point of harming my health or losing control?
Am I too demanding with food, causing trouble for those who prepare it?
Have I spent excessive money on feasts or drinks while others go hungry?
Do I let my cravings rule me, never practicing fasting or abstinence?

categories.name: Wrath
categories.focus: The disordered desire for revenge or uncontrolled anger.
questions: Have I reacted with physical or verbal violence toward others?
Do I hold resentment and refuse to forgive from the heart?
Have I been impatient and irritable with my family or coworkers?
Do I use sarcasm or silence to punish others?

categories.name: Sloth
categories.focus: Lack of effort in fulfilling one's duties.
questions: Have I neglected my daily prayer out of laziness or distraction?
Do I arrive late to Mass or my commitments because of a lack of discipline?
Do I waste too much time on social media or television, neglecting my duties?
Have I let opportunities to do good pass by, simply to avoid discomfort?

alternative_exams.title: Examen of the Virtues (Theological and Cardinal)
alternative_exams.description: A deep look at how we are cultivating the virtues that guide our Christian life.

categories.name: Faith
categories.focus: Believing in God and in everything He has revealed.
questions: Have I neglected the study of my faith, letting my doubts grow without seeking answers?
Am I ashamed of being Catholic in social or professional settings?
Have I placed my trust in horoscopes, divination, or philosophies contrary to the Gospel?

categories.name: Hope
categories.focus: Trusting in the promises of Christ and in eternal life.
questions: Have I fallen into despair, doubting God's mercy in the face of my sins?
Have I sinned by presumption, thinking I can be saved without effort or without grace?
Do I complain constantly about my situation, failing to trust in divine Providence?

categories.name: Charity
categories.focus: Loving God above all things and our neighbor as ourselves.
questions: Is God the center of my life, or do I only seek Him when I have problems?
Have I been indifferent to the suffering of others, especially those closest to me?
Have I let selfishness guide my decisions instead of service?

categories.name: Prudence
categories.focus: Discerning our true good and choosing the right means to attain it.
questions: Do I make important decisions without praying or seeking wise counsel?
Have I acted impulsively, hurting others with my words or actions?
Have I been negligent in my daily responsibilities?

categories.name: Justice
categories.focus: Giving God and neighbor what is due to them.
questions: Have I been honest in my business dealings, taxes, and in the use of time at work?
Have I paid fairly those who provide me a service?
Have I judged others without grounds, or taken part in gossip?

categories.name: Fortitude
categories.focus: Firmness in difficulties and constancy in the pursuit of the good.
questions: Do I give up easily in the face of temptation or life's trials?
Have I been afraid to defend the truth out of fear of rejection?
Am I lazy in fulfilling my spiritual and family duties?

categories.name: Temperance
categories.focus: Moderating the attraction of pleasures and seeking balance.
questions: Have I abused food, alcohol, social media, or entertainment?
Do I find it hard to control my impulses or temper when things don't go my way?
Have I been vain, always seeking to be the center of attention?

alternative_exams.title: Examen of the Works of Mercy
alternative_exams.description: A review based on our active response to the needs of others, following Christ's command.

categories.name: Corporal Works of Mercy
categories.focus: Attending to the physical needs of our neighbor.
questions: Have I walked past someone who was hungry or thirsty when I could have helped?
Have I been generous with clothing or goods I no longer use, or do I hoard them needlessly?
Have I taken time to visit the sick or someone who is alone?
Am I hospitable to those who arrive in my community or home?
Have I helped someone in financial difficulty, asking nothing in return?

categories.name: Spiritual Works of Mercy
categories.focus: Attending to the needs of the soul and spirit.
questions: Have I given good counsel to someone who needed it, or stayed silent out of fear or laziness?
Have I been patient with others' faults, or do I react with bitterness?
Have I consoled those who are sad or going through grief?
Have I forgiven offenses from the heart, or do I keep feeding my resentment?
Have I prayed for the living and the dead, including those who have hurt me?
Have I tried to teach or share my faith with those who do not know God?

closing.scripture: Truly I tell you, whatever you did for one of the least of these brothers of mine, you did for me. (Matthew 25:40)

alternative_exams.title: Daily Examen (St. Ignatius of Loyola)
alternative_exams.description: A daily review of the heart to find God in all things and to prepare for tomorrow.

categories.name: 1. Thanksgiving
categories.focus: Give thanks to God our Lord for the benefits received.
questions: What three moments of grace or joy did I experience today?
Did I thank God for the gift of life, health, and work at the start of the day?
Did I recognize God's presence in the people I met today?

categories.name: 2. Petition for Light
categories.focus: Ask for grace to know my sins and to cast them from me.
questions: Have I asked the Holy Spirit to help me see my day with His eyes, not only with my own?
Am I willing to look at my truth with humility and without self-deception?

categories.name: 3. Review of the Day
categories.focus: Examine the soul from the hour of rising to the present examen.
questions: How did I react to today's challenges or moments of stress?
Was I faithful to my commitments of prayer and service?
At what moments did I stray from God's love (thoughts, words, or omissions)?
Was there a dominant feeling today (sadness, anxiety, joy) and why?

categories.name: 4. Ask for Forgiveness
categories.focus: Ask God's forgiveness for the day's faults.
questions: Have I sincerely asked God's forgiveness for my falls today?
Do I trust in His infinite mercy, or do I stay trapped in guilt?
Have I forgiven those who annoyed or hurt me throughout the day?

categories.name: 5. Resolution for Amendment
categories.focus: Resolve to amend, with His grace.
questions: What specific aspect do I want to improve tomorrow with God's help?
Is there a difficult situation tomorrow for which I need a special grace?
How can I be more aware of God's presence in tomorrow's routine?

closing.prayer: Our Father, who art in heaven... (Pray an Our Father to close the day).

alternative_exams.title: Examen of Love (1 Corinthians 13)
alternative_exams.description: A deep reflection based on the qualities of true love described by Saint Paul.

categories.name: Patience and Kindness
categories.focus: Love is patient, love is kind.
questions: Have I lost patience with the weaknesses of those around me?
Am I attentive and anticipate others' needs, or do I wait until they ask for help?
Have I been rude or sharp in my speech today?

categories.name: Humility and Generosity
categories.focus: Love is not envious, not boastful, not proud.
questions: Have I bragged about my achievements to feel superior to others?
Have I felt envy toward another's success or peace?
Have I acted selfishly, seeking only my own interest or comfort?

categories.name: Forgiveness and Meekness
categories.focus: Love is not easily angered, it keeps no record of wrongs.
questions: Have I reacted with disproportionate anger to an inconvenience?
Do I carry a mental 'list' of the offenses done to me (keeping a record of wrongs)?
Have I been quick to forgive, or do I let the sun set on my anger?

categories.name: Truth and Hope
categories.focus: Love rejoices in the truth. It bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things.
questions: Have I secretly rejoiced at the failure or mistake of someone I dislike?
Have I defended the truth with charity, or used it as a weapon to wound?
Have I lost hope in people, treating them as though they could never change?
Have I been able to bear difficulties with faith, without complaining bitterly?

closing.scripture: And now these three remain: faith, hope, and love. But the greatest of these is love. (1 Corinthians 13:13)

alternative_exams.title: Examen of the Gifts of the Holy Spirit
alternative_exams.description: A review of how we allow God's grace to act within our intellect and will.

categories.name: Wisdom and Understanding
categories.focus: Seeing things as God sees them.
questions: Do I seek God's will before making important decisions, or am I impulsive?
Do I try to deepen in the truths of the faith, or settle for superficial knowledge?
Do I stop to meditate on the Word of God so that it may illumine my daily life?

categories.name: Counsel and Fortitude
categories.focus: Acting with righteousness and courage.
questions: Do I ask the Holy Spirit for counsel when I am confused?
Have I had the strength to say 'no' to sin in moments of strong temptation?
Am I constant in my spiritual resolutions, or do I give up at the first obstacle?

categories.name: Piety and Fear of God
categories.focus: A filial relationship with the Father.
questions: Do I treat God as a loving Father, or as a distant and cold judge?
Do I have deep respect for sacred things (the Eucharist, the temple, prayer)?
Do I avoid sin out of love for God, or only out of fear of punishment?

alternative_exams.title: Examen for Young People
alternative_exams.description: A practical guide for young people seeking to live their faith at school, with friends, and at home.

categories.name: At Home and at School
categories.focus: Honoring parents and being responsible.
questions: Have I disobeyed my parents or talked back with disrespect?
Do I help with chores at home without being asked a thousand times?
Have I been honest in my studies, or have I cheated on tests and assignments?
Do I waste too much time on video games or my phone, neglecting my duties?

categories.name: Friendships and Peer Pressure
categories.focus: Being a light among friends.
questions: Have I joined in teasing or bullying toward a classmate?
Have I let friends pressure me into things I know are wrong (alcohol, drugs, sex)?
Have I stood up for someone who was being mistreated, or stayed silent out of fear?
Do I speak ill of my friends behind their backs?

alternative_exams.title: Examen of the Sanctification of Work
alternative_exams.description: A guide to evaluate our integrity, leadership, and purpose at work and in business.

categories.name: Integrity and Honesty
categories.focus: Reflecting the truth of Christ in every transaction and decision.
questions: Have I been honest in my reports, billing, taxes, and handling of others' resources?
Have I kept my promises and contracts, even when it was costly or difficult?
Have I used my position or authority for personal benefit at the expense of others?
Have I spoken ill of the competition or of colleagues to raise my own image?

categories.name: Excellence and Diligence
categories.focus: Doing the work well for the love of God, not merely to get it done.
questions: Do I strive to do my work with the highest possible quality, as if doing it for the Lord?
Have I wasted time at work on personal distractions (social media, leisure)?
Have I neglected my professional or technical development, limiting my ability to serve better?
Have I been orderly and responsible with the tools and work environment entrusted to me?

categories.name: Leadership and Charity
categories.focus: Treating colleagues, clients, and subordinates with dignity.
questions: Do I treat my employees or collaborators with justice, paying what is due and respecting their rest?
Have I been a leader who serves, or a boss who oppresses and humiliates?
Have I let the stress of work affect my kindness toward others?
Have I borne witness to my Christian values in my professional setting without fear of judgment?

categories.name: Balance and Detachment
categories.focus: Not letting work become an idol.
questions: Have I let ambition or professional success become my priority over God and family?
Am I able to disconnect from work to be truly present at home?
Have I worked excessively out of anxiety or a lack of trust in divine Providence?
Do I accept professional failures with humility and successes with gratitude, without inflating my ego?

alternative_exams.title: Examen for Family Life
alternative_exams.description: A guide to review our vocation as pillars of the Christian home.

categories.name: As Spouse
categories.focus: The sacrament of marriage as a path to holiness.
questions: Have I placed my spouse as my human priority, after God?
Have I been grateful for the small sacrifices my spouse makes for me?
Have I held grudges or brought up past offenses instead of forgiving?
Have I neglected deep conversation, preferring my phone or the television?
Have I been faithful not only physically, but also in my thoughts and desires?

categories.name: As Parent
categories.focus: The responsibility of educating and loving our children.
questions: Have I been an example of faith and prayer for my children?
Have I taken time to listen to them and know their concerns, or only to correct them?
Have I lost my temper and shouted at them, or treated them with unnecessary harshness?
Do I work to create an atmosphere of joy and peace at home?
Have I neglected my own formation so as to answer their questions about life and faith?

categories.name: Stewardship of the Home
categories.focus: The use of resources and time as a family.
questions: Have I let work or outside business consume the time that belongs to my family?
Are we a family open to the needs of other relatives or neighbors?
Have I encouraged the reading of the Word or the praying of the Rosary at home?

closing.act_of_contrition: O my God, I am heartily sorry for having offended Thee, and I detest all my sins because of thy just punishments, but most of all because they offend Thee, my God, who art all good and deserving of all my love. I firmly resolve with the help of Thy grace to sin no more and to avoid the near occasion of sin. Amen.
